title: Texte du memorandum (Protocole d'accord entre le ministère de l'Europe et des affaires étrangères de la République française et le ministère des affaires étrangères du gouvernement du Népal pour la mise en place d'un mécanisme de consultation bilatérale)

1

TRAUT o a AC AA A e TENAN pA Mime Magt y ng ant MM pini pme et ae Rag tpai i e mear mea epe ta an ` . oe

MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING

BETWEEN

THE MINISTRY OF EUROPE AND FOREIGN AFFAIRS OF

THE REPUBLIC OF FRANCE

• AND

THE MINISTRY OF FOREIGN AFFAIRS OF

THE GOVERNMENT OF NEPAL

ON

THE ESTABLISHMENT OF A BILATERAL CONSULTATION MECHANISM

The Ministry of Europe and Foreign Affairs of the Republic of France and the Ministry of

Foreign Affairs of the Goverment of Nepal hereafter referred to as the "Parties" collectively and 'Party' individually,

Being desirous of strengthening the existing relations between the two countries,

Determined to enhance bilateral consultations and exchanges of information in all areas of mutual cooperation,

Convinced that a sincere and constructive dialogue on all aspects of the bilateral relations and on international issues of common interest will contribute to a better mutual understanding to further development of friendly relations berween the two countries,

Have reached the following understanding:

Article 1

a. The objective of this Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) is to establish a bilateral consultations mechanism for the Parties.

b. In order to carry out their consultations, the Parties will hold meetings to review their bilateral relations and to exchange views on regional and international issues of common interest.

Article 2

The bilateral consultations meetings shall be held alternatively in Kathmandu and Paris annually at mutually convenient dates determined through diplomatic channels.

Article 3

The consultative meetings shall be held at the Foreign Secretary or Director

General/Joint Secretary level.

1

Article 4

The agenda of the meeting will be jointly determined in advance through diplomatic

• channels.

Article 5

This MoU may be amended upon mutual consent of the Parties.

Article 6

This MoU will come into effect on the date of its signature by the Parties and will remain valid for a period of 5 (five) years. It will be automatically extended for consecutive periods of 5 (five) years unless either Party notifies in writing otherwise.

Either Party may terminate this MoU by giving 6 (six) months' prior notice to the other

Party through diplomatic channels.

Done in Kathmandu on 13 December 2019 in two originals, each in English language.

For

The Ministry of Europe and Foreign Affairs

The Republic of France

Francois- Xavier Leger

Ambassador of France to Nepal

For

The Ministry of Foreign Affairs

The Government of Nepal

Pable:

Nirmal Raj Kafte

Joint Secretary

2
